sources.title: Wing skin material change: CFRP → hybrid carbon-aramid composite (P-1008, P-1009)
sources.body:
ECR-207 | Wing skin material change: CFRP → hybrid carbon-aramid composite (P-1008, P-1009)

Engineering Change Request ECR-207.

Raised by: Tobias Wren (Structural/Airframe Engineer), 2023-08-29.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Dr. Rhea Voss, 2023-09-05.

Summary: Change wing skin material for P-1008 (port) and P-1009 (starboard) from CFRP (0°/90° layup) to hybrid carbon-aramid composite (CFRP weave + Kevlar® ±45° interlayer).

Rationale: Ground vibration testing (June 2023) identified delamination at the wing root bond line of both CFRP wing skins (P-1008, P-1009) after 12 hours at the motor vibration spectrum, documented in KES-045 (delamination bug) under the KES-042 wing skin trade study epic.

Material coupon tests in KES-042 evaluated three options: CFRP (failed at 12h), quasi-isotropic CFRP (passes 50h estimate), and hybrid carbon-aramid (no delamination at 100h). The aramid ±45° interlayer provides crack arrest and dramatically increases impact resistance. This was agreed at WIKI-017 (Airframe Materials Review, 2023-08-22). The mass penalty is +25g per skin (+50g total), which is within the 340g system mass margin documented in REV-631.

Impact:
- P-1008: Material updated to hybrid carbon-aramid composite, Rev C
- P-1009: Material updated to hybrid carbon-aramid composite, Rev C
- DOC-409: Structural Specification to be updated
- DOC-407: Airframe Loads Analysis to be re-run for stiffness change (Tobias Wren)
- DRW-502: Wing Skin & Spar Assembly drawing to be updated with new layup schedule
- P-1017 (wiring harness bracket): No change — bracket bonded to airframe, not wing skin

sources.title: Approved Change — ECR-207: Wing skin material change to hybrid carbon-aramid composite
sources.body:
ECN-307 | Approved Change — ECR-207: Wing skin material change to hybrid carbon-aramid composite

Engineering Change Notice ECN-307. Implements ECR-207.
Approved by: Dr. Rhea Voss, 2023-09-05.

Authorised changes:
1. P-1008 (Wing Skin Port): Updated to Rev C. Material changed to hybrid carbon-aramid composite (CFRP weave + Kevlar® ±45° interlayer). Mass increase: +25g.
2. P-1009 (Wing Skin Starboard): Updated to Rev C. Same material change. Mass increase: +25g.
3. DOC-409 (Structural Specification): Updated to include hybrid composite material specification and bonding requirements.
4. DOC-407 (Airframe Loads Analysis): Re-run for modified stiffness characteristics of hybrid composite. Stiffness reduction of 8% vs straight CFRP confirmed acceptable against flutter margins. Tobias Wren, completed 2023-10-05.
5. DRW-502 (Wing Skin & Spar Assembly): Updated with hybrid composite layup schedule (0°/90° CFRP + ±45° aramid interlayer + 0°/90° CFRP).
6. Mass impact: +50g total. System mass margin remains at 290g (340g - 50g), per updated DOC-401.

sources.title: K-200 Structural Specification v2.0
sources.body:
DOC-409 | K-200 Structural Specification v2.0

# K-200 Structural Specification

Document number: MD-DOC-409-B. Revision: B. Type: specification. Author: Tobias Wren. Created: 2023-04-10. Last modified: 2023-10-15. Status: Released.

## Change History
- Rev A: Initial structural spec, CFRP wing skins (2023-04-01)
- Rev B: Wing skin material change to hybrid carbon-aramid composite per ECN-307 / ECR-207 (2023-10-15)

## §3 Wing Skin (P-1008, P-1009)

Material: Hybrid carbon-aramid composite — CFRP weave (0°/90°) + Kevlar® ±45° interlayer + CFRP weave (0°/90°). Total thickness: 1.4mm nominal.

Rationale for material change: Straight CFRP (0°/90°) wing skins (P-1008/P-1009 Rev B) delaminated at the wing root bond line after 12 hours of simulated motor vibration loading during ground vibration test (KES-045). Material coupon tests in KES-042 confirmed hybrid carbon-aramid has no delamination at 100 hours (vs CFRP failure at 12 hours). The aramid interlayer provides crack arrest. Mass penalty: +25g per skin. Design review: WIKI-017. Change authority: ECN-307.

The stiffness of the hybrid composite is approximately 8% lower than straight CFRP. Loads analysis re-run in DOC-407 confirmed this reduction is acceptable against flutter margins, with flutter onset at 52m/s (requirement: >45m/s at maximum dive speed).

## §4 Motor Mounts (P-1012–P-1015)

Aluminium 6061-T6. Stiffness requirement: natural frequency of motor mount assembly >200Hz (to avoid coupling with motor vibration spectrum). Verified in DRW-507.
